member.name: M:Obsolete("Obsolete")
member.summary: Execute an Ethereum transaction on Hedera

member.name: F:.MAX_ETHEREUM_DATA_SIZE
member.summary: 128,000 bytes - jumbo transaction limit Indicates when we should splice out the call data from an ethereum transaction data

member.name: P:.EthereumData
member.summary: Sets the raw Ethereum transaction (RLP encoded type 0, 1, and 2). Complete unless the callDataFileId is set.

member.name: P:.MaxGasAllowance
member.summary:
Sets the maximum amount that the payer of the hedera transaction is willing to pay to complete the transaction.
Ordinarily the account with the ECDSA alias corresponding to the public key that is extracted from the ethereum_data signature is responsible for fees that result from the execution of the transaction. If that amount of authorized fees is not sufficient then the payer of the transaction can be charged, up to but not exceeding this amount. If the ethereum_data transaction authorized an amount that was insufficient then the payer will only be charged the amount needed to make up the difference. If the gas price in the transaction was set to zero then the payer will be assessed the entire fee.

member.name: M:Execute(Client)
member.summary: Execute the transactions in the flow with the passed in client.
member.param: the client with the transaction to execute

member.name: M:Execute(Client,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Execute the transactions in the flow with the passed in client.
member.param: the client with the transaction to execute
The timeout after which each transaction's execution attempt will be cancelled.

member.name: M:ExecuteAsync(Client)
member.summary:
Execute the transactions in the flow with the passed in client asynchronously.
Note: This method requires API level 33 or higher. It will not work on devices running API versions below 31 because it uses features introduced in API level 31 (Android 12).*
member.param: the client with the transaction to execute

member.name: M:ExecuteAsync(Client,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary:
Execute the transactions in the flow with the passed in client asynchronously.
Note: This method requires API level 33 or higher. It will not work on devices running API versions below 31 because it uses features introduced in API level 31 (Android 12).*
member.param: the client with the transaction to execute
The timeout after which each transaction's execution attempt will be cancelled.

member.name: M:ExecuteAsync(Client,System.Action{TransactionResponse,System.Exception})
member.summary: Execute the transactions in the flow with the passed in client asynchronously.
member.param: the client with the transaction to execute
a Action which handles the result or error.

member.name: M:ExecuteAsync(Client,System.TimeSpan,System.Action{TransactionResponse,System.Exception})
member.summary: Execute the transactions in the flow with the passed in client asynchronously.
member.param: the client with the transaction to execute
The timeout after which each transaction's execution attempt will be cancelled.
a Action which handles the result or error.

member.name: M:ExecuteAsync(Client,System.Action{TransactionResponse},System.Action{System.Exception})
member.summary: Execute the transactions in the flow with the passed in client asynchronously.
member.param: the client with the transaction to execute
a Action which consumes the result on success.
a Action which consumes the error on failure.

member.name: M:ExecuteAsync(Client,System.TimeSpan,System.Action{TransactionResponse},System.Action{System.Exception})
member.summary: Execute the transactions in the flow with the passed in client asynchronously.
member.param: the client with the transaction to execute
The timeout after which each transaction's execution attempt will be cancelled.
a Action which consumes the result on success.
a Action which consumes the error on failure.
